observation.summary: The local daemon implementation contract described runtime pools, task DB sidecars, leases, and fencing as prose while the proof lived across scheduling, supervisor, taskdbplane, and cmd/riido files.

hypothesis.summary: A manifest can generate the reader doc and prove runtime-scoped claim dispatch, pure selector boundaries, sidecar registry ownership, and lease fencing from source checks.

execution.summary: Run localdaemoncontract to check generated docs, source checks, absent adapter/persistence imports in scheduling, and absent provider execution imports in taskdbplane.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects generated doc drift, missing source evidence, unknown fact references, scheduler boundary leaks, and taskdbplane provider execution leaks.

retrospective.summary: Local daemon scheduling knowledge becomes executable contract evidence instead of a manually edited implementation note.

evidence.proves: local daemon contract manifest matches generated docs, implementation references, and absent boundary rules
public CI uploads local daemon contract evidence
